positioning: Fixed-price automation for UK ecommerce and wholesale teams, built by an operator who ran a 200,000 SKU multi-channel ecommerce operation.

streams.name: Commercial SEO
streams.purpose: Capture problem-aware searches from ecommerce and wholesale teams looking to automate supplier files, reports, stock sync, listings and invoice admin.
example_topics: How to automate supplier price lists for Shopify and Amazon
What to automate before hiring another ecommerce administrator
How UK wholesalers can stop re-keying orders into Sage or Xero
Why your weekly sales report should not be built by hand

streams.name: Founder-led operator notes
streams.purpose: Build trust and authority using Henry Oliver's real experience running a 200,000 SKU ecommerce operation.
example_topics: What I learned running 200,000 SKUs across Shopify, Amazon and eBay
The hidden cost of fixing the spreadsheet every week
The difference between a software problem and a process problem
Why ROI maths should come before an automation build

quality_rules: Write for UK ecommerce and wholesale operators first, not search engines.
No filler, generic automation hype or repeated points.
Do not invent client results, statistics, dates, prices or case studies.
Every article must include a clear decision point, operational example or ROI calculation where relevant.
Avoid template-like card excerpts on the blog index.
Update sitemap.xml, llms.txt where useful and this content plan after every published article.
